sectionOrderRules: Hero must lead with the studio's voice — bold typography, declarative tagline, no stock-business filler.
Selected work preview must come before any service or capability copy. Agencies sell on the work, not the services list.
Manifesto block reads as the studio's point of view, not a tagline — full-width statement, no decoration.
Selected work grid must dominate — one card per project, large-enough type to read on a 13-inch laptop.
Client roster acts as recognition signal, not a logo wall — text-only pill row is enough.
Story must be specific (founder names, year founded, where the studio's voice came from), not generic 'we believe great design changes things' filler.
Team must use first-name-first informality — agencies are people-led, not partner-led.
Process steps name the studio's actual stages with words a producer recognises (Discovery, Concept, Production, Launch).
Phone is a tel: link and email is a mailto: link.
Address renders as a single readable block so a producer can paste it into a calendar invite.
